text: It is not clear where the comments of the supposed passer-by come to an end.
No or No-Ammon is commonly identified with (Egyptian) Thebes, the ‘sea’ being understood as referring to the Nile. The Latin version gives ‘Alexandria’, which involves a manifest anachronism.
In the Hebrew text, ‘thy merchants’. A change of one letter would give ‘thy foot-soldiers’.

The words rendered ‘high lords’ and ‘princelings’ are rare words of uncertain significance. The Latin has ‘guardians’ and ‘little ones’, but it seems likely that state officials of some kind are meant.
